{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Growth Marketing Manager",
  "description": "EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Growth Marketing Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Our company is seeking a skilled Growth Marketing Manager to lead our digital marketing initiatives and drive significant growth.


Responsibilities


	Manage and optimize performance of digital marketing channels.
	Forecast, measure, and analyze digital campaign performance across the funnel.
	Develop and implement digital demand growth strategies.
	Partner with cross-functional teams for trial conversion and activation rate improvement.
	Guide paid efforts across channels, balancing growth and ad spend.
	Oversee ad unit development, testing, and analysis for continuous improvement.


Requirements


	Expertise in Paid Search, Paid Social, Display, Retargeting, Affiliate, CRO, and SEO.
	Familiarity with acquisition analysis, A/B testing, and ad fraud detection.
	Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
	Up-to-date knowledge of mobile performance marketing, trends, and best practices.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;integratedresourcesemadorniger@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Growth Marketing Manager Job at EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources




        
    

        
        EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Growth Marketing Manager
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our company is seeking a skilled Growth Marketing Manager to lead our digital marketing initiatives and drive significant growth.


Responsibilities


	Manage and optimize performance of digital marketing channels.
	Forecast, measure, and analyze digital campaign performance across the funnel.
	Develop and implement digital demand growth strategies.
	Partner with cross-functional teams for trial conversion and activation rate improvement.
	Guide paid efforts across channels, balancing growth and ad spend.
	Oversee ad unit development, testing, and analysis for continuous improvement.


Requirements


	Expertise in Paid Search, Paid Social, Display, Retargeting, Affiliate, CRO, and SEO.
	Familiarity with acquisition analysis, A/B testing, and ad fraud detection.
	Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
	Up-to-date knowledge of mobile performance marketing, trends, and best practices.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Manager",
  "description": "Resource Intermediaries Limited (RIL) is a company registered in Nigeria to engage in outsourcing services. We focus on three key areas; Human Resource Management, People Placement and Learning &amp; Development. We provide a broad menu of Human Resource Outsourcing Services for companies from an economical complete service.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Sales Manager to lead revenue growth, expand market share, and manage a high-performing sales team across Lagos.
	The ideal candidate combines strong leadership, market insight, and a proven track record of exceeding ambitious sales targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute data-driven sales strategies to exceed revenue targets.
	Lead, coach, and scale a high-performing sales team across Lagos
	Drive acquisition of key accounts and strategic partnerships
	Monitor sales performance metrics and optimize conversion and ROI
	Identify and convert new market opportunities into revenue streams
	Manage pipeline forecasting, reporting, and performance tracking
	Build strong relationships with clients, vendors, and stakeholders.


Requirements


	HND/BSc in Business, Marketing, or related field (MBA is an advantage)
	8&ndash;12 years pf sales experience, with 4+ years in a leadership role
	Proven record of consistently exceeding sales targets
	Strong leadership, negotiation, and communication skills
	Analytical mindset with strong use of CRM tools and sales analytics
	Strong knowledge of Lagos market dynamics (Island &amp; Mainland).


What We Offer


	Salary: N600,000 per month.
	Highly competitive salary package
	Performance-based bonuses and incentives
	Opportunity to lead and shape a growing sales function
	Career growth in a fast-paced, innovative environment.

Sales Manager Job at Resource Intermediaries Limited




        
    

        
        Resource Intermediaries Limited (RIL) is a company registered in Nigeria to engage in outsourcing services. We focus on three key areas; Human Resource Management, People Placement and Learning & Development. We provide a broad menu of Human Resource Outsourcing Services for companies from an economical complete service.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Sales Manager to lead revenue growth, expand market share, and manage a high-performing sales team across Lagos.
	The ideal candidate combines strong leadership, market insight, and a proven track record of exceeding ambitious sales targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute data-driven sales strategies to exceed revenue targets.
	Lead, coach, and scale a high-performing sales team across Lagos
	Drive acquisition of key accounts and strategic partnerships
	Monitor sales performance metrics and optimize conversion and ROI
	Identify and convert new market opportunities into revenue streams
	Manage pipeline forecasting, reporting, and performance tracking
	Build strong relationships with clients, vendors, and stakeholders.


Requirements


	HND/BSc in Business, Marketing, or related field (MBA is an advantage)
	8–12 years pf sales experience, with 4+ years in a leadership role
	Proven record of consistently exceeding sales targets
	Strong leadership, negotiation, and communication skills
	Analytical mindset with strong use of CRM tools and sales analytics
	Strong knowledge of Lagos market dynamics (Island & Mainland).


What We Offer


	Salary: N600,000 per month.
	Highly competitive salary package
	Performance-based bonuses and incentives
	Opportunity to lead and shape a growing sales function
	Career growth in a fast-paced, innovative environment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Safety and Security",
  "description": "Lindii Peace Foundation&nbsp;is a nonprofit non-governmental national organization established in Nigeria in July 2014 with the aim to improve the lives of people through effective and efficient humanitarian and developmental assistance in communities where such supports are essentially necessary within Nigeria. Through international and national networking, LPF provided humanitarian and developmental assistance to individuals, families, schools, Health facilities, and communities in more than twelve (12) states within Nigeria regardless of their ethnicity, political and religious affiliation. LPF fundamentally intervenes in multi-sectoral humanitarian activities majoring in Protection, Health, Food Security &amp; Livelihood, Education, Nutrition, Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) interventions, and other Cross-Sectoral areas.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Safety and Security
Job Location:&nbsp;Maiduguri (Roving BAY States) - Borno
Employment Type: Contract

Duty Description


	The security Officer is responsible for ensuring the safety of all LPF personnel and LPF Properties. He is responsible for mission clearance for staff going to the field, He coordinates with the Field Base Local Security formation, INGO Forum, and UNDSS to gather information about the security situation of the highly threatened location in the BAY State.
	The purpose of this role is to ensure LPF working environment is consistent with accepted minimum safety and security standards.
	This encompasses the development of security plans including mainstreaming individual and collective sense of security and safety awareness and responsibility, developing risk assessments, and proposing risk reduction measures, security protocols, and operating procedures according to the changing situation


Job Description
Environment Analysis:


	Analyse the parameters of the working environment by assessing the nature of the threats and LPF vulnerabilities in relation to the emergency situation.
	Collect the necessary data and information to develop a comprehensive risk assessment.
	Monitor political, social, economic, and military situations and identify trends relevant to security situation.


Security management:
Prevention:


	Contribute to developing a safety and security culture within the organization by addressing and helping staff to address safety and security-related issues.
	Ensure that security planning becomes an integral part of day-to-day operations and is integrated into the development of future programs.
	Assist with the strategic and operational analysis and assessments and with the refinement of program strategies.
	Perform security risk assessments or evaluation, review safety and security procedures of the organization and its implementation location regularly, meeting minimum standards and complying with policies and procedures.
	Develop and regularly update the Safety and Security Management Plan and tools in accordance with the nature of safety and security risks (including. detailed threat assessment, security protocols, standard operating procedures for personal movement &amp; transport, office, warehouse, tele/radio communications, incident report sheets, etc asset protection).
	Ensure safety and security dimensions are factored into all operational activities such as programming (including budget), advocacy, and communication.
	Assist managers in enhancing standards in relation to safety and security
	Coordinate with the Human Resources / Administration Department the security officer will ensure field staff know how to identify health risks in the region, how to protect against illness, injury, and stress, and how to obtain proper support or medical treatment.
	Work with administrative / logistics staff to ensure appropriate steps are taken to safeguard office premises, equipment, and residential property (guards, locks, lighting, alarms, and fire equipment).
	Develop a network of contacts among local actors to facilitate LPF work and enhance the security of its operations.
	Represent LPF and coordinate amongst all actors (state, military, Partners, NGO, etc) on all matters pertaining to safety and security.
	Organize and circulate relevant safety and security information to the staff and provide an opportunity for employees to voice Safety and/or Security-related concerns.
	Contribute to the security of the humanitarian community by sharing relevant security related information
	Ensure timely and accurate reporting of safety and security incidents.
	Coordinate training on safety and security ensuring that staff have the necessary equipment and are trained accordingly.
	Ensure that project proposals and operational plans budgets include safety and security plans.
	Ensure that all incoming visitors receive a security briefing and information.
	Ensure all staff understand and fulfill individual and collective responsibilities for safety and security.
	Supervise and enforce (including disciplinary action where necessary) compliance safety and security standards and procedures.


Reaction:


	Ensure that an administrative structure is put in place to effectively deal with crisis management
	Develop contingency plans for incident management events.
	Train staff on contingency measures and ensure they know how to react
	Establish relocation and emergency evacuation plans


Recovery and resumption of operations:


	Ensure that preparations are made to enable post-incident counselling to be offered to staff who have been confronted with traumatic events.
	Take the necessary measures to ensure post-incident recovery and operation continuity.
	Make the necessary arrangements to ensure the resumption of critical processes, systems, and operations (alternate worksites, office storage, and remote control)


Review:


	Review periodically past experiences and practices and propose improvements based on analytical judgment.


Reporting Lines:


	This position reports to LPF Country Director.


Selection Criteria Core Competencies:
Skills:


	Ability to work independently and as a team player who demonstrates leadership and is able to support and train staff and also able to work with disaster-affected communities in a sensitive and participatory manner.&nbsp;Communication Skills:
	Well-developed written and oral communication skills. Able to communicate clearly and sensitively with internal and external stakeholders as a representative of LPF. This includes effective negotiation and representation skills.


Integrity:


	Works with trustworthiness and integrity and has a clear commitment to LPF&#39;s core values and humanitarian principles.
	Resilience/Adaptability and flexibility: Ability to operate effectively under extreme circumstances including stress, high-security risks, and harsh living conditions. Works and lives in a flexible, adaptable, and resilient manner.
	Awareness and sensitivity of self and others: Demonstrates awareness and sensitivity to gender and diversity. Have experience and the ability to live and work in diverse cultural contexts in a culturally appropriate manner. Has a capacity to make accurate self-assessments, particularly in high-stress and high-security contexts.


Work style:


	Is well-planned and organized even within a fluid working environment and has a capacity for initiative and decision-making with competent analytical and problem-solving skills.


Knowledge and skills:


	knowledge of LPF policies and procedures. Requires general information management and telecommunication skills and proficiency in information technology/ computer skills.
	3&ndash; 5 years of humanitarian aid experience.
	Multiple language skills desirable. Graphics technical competencies required for this position
	High level of experience in developing information and communication related technical tools guidelines and systems.
	Qualifications and / or experience in professional information and communication-related fields.
	Strong assessment, evaluation, analysis, and strategic planning skills.
	Knowledge of Humanitarian /Human rights / Refugee Law.
	Basic knowledge of contract employment and experience and skills in recruitment and selection.


Not e


	Only applicants who scale through the prerequisite conditions will be invited for an interview
	All applicants will be considered based on established merits without regard to race, sex, color, creed, tribe, political affiliation, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable Labor Laws/Acts/Policies.
	Applicants are advised that the recruitement process does not require any form of payment or expenses during the entire process. Any request in this direction should be immediately reported, as it is contrary to established SOPs.
	Due to the volume of applications usually received, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted as at when due.
	Qualified candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible, as applications will be reviewed as received immediately. More so, we still reserve the right to change the closing date, if necessary.

Lindii Peace Foundation is a nonprofit non-governmental national organization established in Nigeria in July 2014 with the aim to improve the lives of people through effective and efficient humanitarian and developmental assistance in communities where such supports are essentially necessary within Nigeria. Through international and national networking, LPF provided humanitarian and developmental assistance to individuals, families, schools, Health facilities, and communities in more than twelve (12) states within Nigeria regardless of their ethnicity, political and religious affiliation. LPF fundamentally intervenes in multi-sectoral humanitarian activities majoring in Protection, Health, Food Security & Livelihood, Education, Nutrition, Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) interventions, and other Cross-Sectoral areas.

title: Job Openings at Lheon Consulting Limited
content:
Lheon Consulting Limited is a strategy development and talent consultancy company, devoted exclusively to career shaping and personal development activities.We work globally with professionals and experts in the financial sectors to offer resourceful training, assessment and recruitment services to financial and non-bank financial institutions through use of innovative technologies to achieve a perfect workplace.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Regional Sales And Activation Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: National Sales Manager
Industry: Wine & Whiskey (FMCG – Alcoholic Beverages)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Regional Sales and Activation Manager will drive sales growth, enhance brand visibility, and expand market penetration for the company’s wine and whiskey portfolio across Lagos and its environs.
	This role requires a commercially driven professional with strong distributor networks, hands-on market execution capability, and proven experience leading high-impact brand activations.


Key Responsibilities
Sales Growth & Market Expansion:


	Develop and implement regional sales strategies to achieve revenue and volume targets.
	Identify and onboard new business opportunities and distribution channels.
	Drive product penetration across supermarkets, bars, lounges, restaurants, and liquor stores.
	Monitor sales performance and implement corrective actions to ensure target achievement.


Brand Activation & Visibility:


	Plan and execute activations, tastings, and promotional campaigns.
	Ensure strong in-market visibility through effective merchandising and POS materials.
	Collaborate with Marketing to localize and execute national campaigns within the region.


Distributor & Channel Management:


	Build and sustain strong relationships with distributors and key trade partners.
	Monitor distributor performance, stock levels, and market coverage.
	Conduct regular market visits to ensure compliance with pricing and brand standards.


Team Leadership & Performance Management:


	Lead Regional Sales Executives, Brand Ambassadors, and Activation Teams.
	Provide coaching, training, and continuous performance management.
	Set clear targets and track team performance against business objectives.


Market Intelligence & Reporting:


	Monitor competitor activities, market trends, and consumer behavior.
	Prepare and submit regular sales and activation reports.
	Leverage insights to refine strategies and strengthen market positioning.


Budget & Resource Management:


	Manage regional sales and activation budgets efficiently.
	Ensure cost-effective execution of campaigns and events.
	Track and report ROI on all sales and activation initiatives.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Revenue and volume growth within the region
	Distribution expansion and outlet coverage
	Effectiveness of brand activations
	Market share growth
	Team productivity and performance
	Distributor efficiency and stock availability.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
	7 - 10 years of experience in sales within FMCG or the beverage/alcohol industry.
	Strong background in brand activation, trade marketing, and distributor management.
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
	Established network of distributors/customers within the industry.
	Requirement: Candidate must own a functional vehicle for mobility.


Salary
N1,000,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Do not apply if you do not have a high network of customers/distributors.

 

 

2.) National Sales Manager / National Coordinator

Job Location: Nigeria (National Coverage)
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director

Job Summary


	The National Sales Manager will be responsible for driving nationwide sales growth, market expansion, and brand visibility for the company’s wine portfolio.
	The role involves developing and executing sales strategies, managing regional sales teams and distributors, and ensuring achievement of revenue targets across all channels.


Key Responsibilities
Sales Strategy & Execution:


	Develop and implement national sales strategies to achieve revenue and volume targets.
	Identify new market opportunities and expand distribution channels.
	Drive penetration in key segments (retail, HORECA – Hotels, Restaurants, Cafés).
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and consumer preferences.


Distribution & Channel Management:


	Manage and strengthen relationships with distributors and key accounts nationwide.
	Ensure optimal product availability and visibility across all regions.
	Negotiate trade terms, pricing, and promotional activities with partners.
	Oversee stock movement and minimize out-of-stock situations.


Team Leadership & Coordination:


	Supervise and coordinate regional sales teams and sales representatives.
	Set KPIs, monitor performance, and provide coaching and support.
	Conduct regular field visits and performance reviews.
	Ensure alignment of regional activities with national objectives.


Brand Development & Marketing Support:


	Collaborate with marketing to execute brand activation campaigns, tastings, and promotions.
	Ensure proper product positioning and brand standards across outlets.
	Support launch of new wine products in the market.


Financial & Reporting:


	Prepare sales forecasts, budgets, and periodic performance reports.
	Track sales performance against targets and implement corrective actions.
	Manage trade spend and ensure cost efficiency.


Key Requirements
Education & Experience:


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field.
	Minimum of 5–8 years of experience in FMCG/beverage sales, preferably in wines or spirits.
	Proven track record of managing national or regional sales operations.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong negotiation and relationship management skills
	Excellent leadership and team management ability
	Deep understanding of distribution networks in Nigeria
	Strong analytical and reporting skills
	Good knowledge of wine products, trends, and customer segments (an added advantage).


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Territory Sales Manager

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Evaluate and monitor outlet and channels profitability.
	Establish the service standard for Mega Accounts, measure efficiency against the standard and close down the gaps.
	In cases of credit customers,be responsible for debt reconciliation and recovery of aged debts
	Do category analysis/brand analysis by Mega Account/Non mega and share the analysis with line managers.
	Have deep knowledge of company products range (SKUs, brands name, flavor, packaging, freshness, prices, company policy in connection to merchandising, product freshness) and communicate same to customers.
	Maximizes all opportunities in the process of closing a sale to a Key Account shopper resulting in the taking of market share from competitors in-store.
	Ensure accurate data capture in Out of Stock and off-take reports and other records to achieve a quality database.
	Share the captured data with HO for analysis, review and improvement purposes.
	Rotate stock at POS and replace overage stocks if necessary.
	Work to a specified plan covering each location in-store/ different stores systematically to improve off-take of all BA products.
	Adherence to work hour schedule and route plan.
	Assists in the implementation of company marketing plans as needed.
	Inform management of any new developments in the store/ stores i.e. competitive activity, new product launch etc.
	Create strong relationship with store-manager, floor operations, supervisors, syndicated merchandisers of the organization and other merchandisers of other companies.
	Representing the company at trade promotions and expo’s
	Direct/guide, control and generally oversee the work of any direct & indirect associate placed under him/her.
	Ensure products are available in good condition, if needed, rotate stock in first-expiry first-out basis (FEFO).
	Ensure his/her planograms are always kept according to instructions.
	Assist and involve in training of new recruits of merchandisers
	Responsible for CREATION of demand from store and NOT JUST SERVICE the demand.


Requirements


	Candidates must have a working vehicle for mobility.
	Candidates must be available to resume immediately.


Remuneration


	Salary: N500,000 monthly.
	Sales Commission: varies based on performance


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Please apply only if you have sold wine and spirit or currently selling wine and spirit.
	We will reach out to only eligible candidates.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Quality Control Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Arepo, Ogun

Job Summary


	The Quality Control Officer is responsible for ensuring that all products, materials, and completed interior d&eacute;cor projects meet the company&rsquo;s quality standards.
	The role involves developing quality policies, monitoring production and installation processes, and ensuring strict adherence to set specifications, especially within an interior d&eacute;cor environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain quality control policies and procedures for interior d&eacute;cor projects and production processes.
	Monitor and evaluate all stages of production, fabrication, and installation to ensure compliance with quality standards.
	Conduct regular inspections of materials, workmanship, and finished products to ensure consistency and excellence.
	Identify quality issues, document findings, and recommend corrective and preventive actions.
	Work closely with production, design, and installation teams to ensure adherence to project specifications.
	Maintain quality records, inspection reports, and performance evaluation documentation.
	Ensure compliance with industry standards and company quality expectations.
	Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance product quality and operational efficiency.


Requirements / Specification


	Proven experience working in an interior d&eacute;cor firm is mandatory
	Strong knowledge of quality control processes and standards
	Experience in monitoring and evaluation of production or project outputs
	Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Ability to enforce quality standards without compromise
	Good communication and reporting skills
	Ability to work onsite and manage multiple project stages


Benefits


	Salary: N150,000 &ndash; N180,000 monthly.
	HMO, Pension.


Method of Application

	The Quality Control Officer is responsible for ensuring that all products, materials, and completed interior décor projects meet the company’s quality standards.
